Submodules

doppler_dimming_lib.codex module

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.convolve_codex_filter(_lambda: float, I, verbose: bool = True) → float

Convolve a function I with the codex filter at _lambda transmissivity. For each available filter transmissivity T, computes the sum of

Parameters:

  • _lambda (float) – filter center in Angstrom.

  • I (callable) – function to convolve, argument is lambda in Armstrong

  • verbose (bool, optional) – print info about computation. Defaults to True.

Returns:

convolution between I(lambda) and the filter transmissivity

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.get_R_as_spline(integral_function, lambda1: float, lambda2: float, **kwargs)

Find the cubic spline interpolator of the ratio function(T1)/function(T2). All the parameters of function must be provided as kwargs.

Parameters:

  • function (callable) – function to perform the ratio numerator/denominator

  • lambda1 (float) – numerator wavelenght in angstrom

  • lambda2 (float) – denominator wavelenght in angstrom

Returns:

spline cubic interpolating 5.e5 < T < 3.e6

Return type:

PPoly

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.get_T_W_from_codex(rho: float, Js: list, p0: list) → list

Uses least squares to find the values of temperature and solar wind that better fit the input Js.

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • Js (list) – codex intesities at the four wavelenghts, ordered

  • p0 (list) – initial guess for temperature and wind speed

Returns:

fitted electron temperature and solar wind speed

Return type:

list

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.get_T_from_R(s2_over_t2: float, rho: float, wind_speed: float = 0.0, **kwargs)

Retrieve the electron tempearture from a CODEX measured ratio between filters S2 and T2. First gets R as spline for the selected rho, then gets T from the fitted spline.

Parameters:

  • s2_over_t2 (float) – ratio between two filters S2/T2

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • wind_speed (float, optional) – wind speed in km/s. Defaults to 0.

Returns:

inferred electron temperature

Return type:

uncertainties.ufloat

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.set_codex_transmittancies(approx_filter: bool = False, skipevery: int = 10, min_transmittancy: float = 0.001)

Reads the excel file containing the filter transmittancies and sets the dictionary containing arrays of wavelenghts and transmittancies for each filter, ordered by center wavelenght.

Parameters:

  • approx_filter (bool, optional) – approximate filter transmissivity with a box function. This may some time, but results are often inconsistent. Defaults to False.

  • skipevery (int, optional) – sample one transmittance every n entries. Defaults to 10.

  • min_transmittancy (float, optional) – minimum transmittancy to consider, lower ones will be considered 0. Defaults to 0.001 (0.1%).

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.simulate_codex_chunk(densities, z, r_pos, T_e_pos, wind_speed_pos, pid)
doppler_dimming_lib.codex.simulate_codex_filter(filter_name: str, rho: float, T_e: float, wind_speed: float, N_e_function=<function N_e_analytical>, verbose: bool = True) → float

Simulate the measurement from a CODEX filter by convolving its response with Cram, 1976 integral

Parameters:

  • filter_name (str) – filter name, can be T1, S1, T2 or S2

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • T_e (float) – electron temperature in Kelvin

  • wind_speed (float) – wind speed in km/s

  • N_e_function (callable, optional) – electron density as a function of the heliocentric distance (in rsun). Defaults to utils.N_e_analytical.

Returns:

value measured with selected filter in erg cm-2 s-1 sr-1

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.codex.simulate_codex_images(image_side, wind_speed_pos, densities_dc, coordinates_dc, T_e_pos, tot_processors=None)
doppler_dimming_lib.codex.simulate_codex_measure(rho: float, T_e: float, wind_speed: float, N_e_function=<function N_e_analytical>, verbose: bool = True) → ndarray

Simulate the measurement from the four CODEX filters by convolving their response with Cram, 1976 integral

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • T_e (float) – electron temperature in Kelvin

  • wind_speed (float) – wind speed in km/s

  • N_e_function (callable, optional) – electron density as a function of the heliocentric distance (in rsun). Defaults to utils.N_e_analytical.

Returns:

filters measured values, ordered by filter wavelength (T1=393, S1=398, T2=405, S2=423), in erg cm-2 s-1 sr-1

Return type:

np.ndarray(4)

doppler_dimming_lib.datacubes module

doppler_dimming_lib.datacubes.datacube_from_map(_map, **kwargs)
doppler_dimming_lib.datacubes.map_from_ddt(filename: str, hdu_ID: int = 0, resample_to: list = None)

doppler_dimming_lib.integrals module

doppler_dimming_lib.integrals.I_dl_domega_dphi(rho: float, _lambda: float, T_e: float, W: float, verbose: bool = True) → float

Interior integral, separated to allow calculations without integrating in dLOS

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • _lambda (float) – wavelenght in angstrom

  • T_e (float) – electron temperature in Kelvin

  • W (float) – wind speed in km/s

  • verbose (bool, optional) – print additional info at each step for debugging purposes. Defaults to True.

Returns:

photospheric irradiance integrated over solar disc

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.integrals.I_s_lambda(*args, **kwargs) → float

Wrapper for integrated_I_s used to compute total brightness by passing component=3 to integrated_I_s.

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • _lambda (float) – wavelenght in angstrom

  • T_e (float) – electron temperature

  • W (float) – wind speed in km/s

  • min_x (float, optional) – integration limit for LOS away from observer in solar radii. Defaults to 10.

  • max_x (float, optional) – integration limit for LOS towards observer in solar radii. Defaults to 10.

  • N_e_function (callable, optional) – function used to calculate the electron density as a function of heliocentric distance. Defaults to utils.N_e_analytical.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – print additional info at each step. Defaults to True.

Returns:

integrated total brightness

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.integrals.integrated_I_s(rho: float, _lambda: float, T_e: float, wind_speed: float, component: int, min_x: float = None, max_x: float = None, N_e_function=<function N_e_analytical>, verbose=True) → float

Integrate thomson scattering over photospheric wavelenghts, solar disc dimension and line of sight (main integral from Cram 1976.

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • _lambda (float) – wavelenght in angstrom

  • T_e (float) – electron temperature

  • W (float) – wind speed in km/s

  • component (int) – polarized component to compute. With respect to solar limb, 0 is radial, 1 is tangent, 2 is pb (=tangent-radial, Cram 1976) and 3 is the sum (total brightness)

  • min_x (float, optional) – integration limit for LOS away from observer in solar radii. Defaults to 10.

  • max_x (float, optional) – integration limit for LOS towards observer in solar radii. Defaults to 10.

  • N_e_function (callable, optional) – function used to calculate the electron density as a function of heliocentric distance, in cm-3. Defaults to utils.N_e_analytical.

  • verbose (bool, optional) – print additional info at each step. Defaults to True.

Returns:

integrated emission in erg cm-2 A-1 s-1 sr-1

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.inversion module

doppler_dimming_lib.inversion.invert_temperature_batch(codex_images: ndarray, wind_speed_pos: ndarray | float, densities_dc: ndarray, helio_distances_dc: ndarray, pid: list) → ndarray

Get the temperature by inverting CODEX images ratio.

Parameters:

  • codex_images (np.ndarray) – images from CODEX measurements. Should be a [4, m, n] array containing 4 filters measurements [T1, S1, T2, S2] (in order) of shape [m, n].

  • wind_speed_pos (np.ndarray) – [m, n] array or float containing the wind speed intensity in the POS for each pixel of the CODEX measurements. If a float is provided, homogeneous wind speed is assumed.

  • densities_dc (np.ndarray) – datacube of shape [l, m, n] containing electron densities in cm^-3. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

  • helio_distances_dc (np.ndarray) – datacube of shape [l, m, n] containing heliocentric distances of each density datacube entry in solar radii. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

  • pid (list) – id used for printing progress.

Returns:

temperatures resulting from inversion in the POS, in Kelvin.

Return type:

np.ndarray

doppler_dimming_lib.inversion.invert_temperature_parallel(codex_images: ndarray, wind_speed_pos: ndarray, densities_dc: ndarray, coordinates_dc: ndarray, total_processors: int = None) → ndarray

Get the temperature by inverting CODEX images ratio. This version first splits the domain for X processors and then applies invert_temperature_batch to each subdomain, before merging the result.

Parameters:

  • codex_images (np.ndarray) – images from CODEX measurements. Should be a [4, m, n] array containing 4 filters measurements [T1, S1, T2, S2] (in order) of shape [m, n].

  • wind_speed_pos (np.ndarray) – [m, n] array or float containing the wind speed intensity in the POS for each pixel of the CODEX measurements. If a float is provided, homogeneous wind speed is assumed.

  • densities_dc (np.ndarray) – datacube of shape [l, m, n] containing electron densities in cm^-3. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

  • coordinates_dc (np.ndarray) – datacube of shape [l, m, n] containing coordinates of each density datacube entry in solar radii. The first index contains values along the LOS for each pixel, and POS is assumed to be at int(l/2).

  • total_processors (int, optional) – number of processors for parallelization. This number should be tweaked to avoid asymmetric division of the domain which can increase the computation time. If set to None, 4 processors are used. Defaults to None.

Returns:

temperatures resulting from inversion in the POS, in Kelvin.

Return type:

np.ndarray

doppler_dimming_lib.metis module

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.Ne_exponential_fit(x: float, b: ndarray) → float

Fitted ne based on metis pB.

Parameters:

  • x (float) – heliocentric radial distance in Rsun

  • b (np.ndarray) – coefficients array

Returns:

electron density at a given heliocentric radial distance

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.Te_pos_from_ne(filename, image_side)
doppler_dimming_lib.metis.datacube_from_map(ne_map: GenericMap, coefficients: ndarray) → ndarray
doppler_dimming_lib.metis.density_dc_from_ddt(filename: str, size_in_pixel: int) → ndarray

Get the electron density datacube from fitted metis values.

Parameters:

  • filename (str) – fits filename of the metis VL pb.

  • size_in_pixel (int) – output datacube size

Returns:

electron density datacube of size [size_in_pixel, size_in_pixel, size_in_pixel]

Return type:

np.ndarray

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.geom_factor_function(r: float) → float

Geometric factor function from Van de Hulst.

Parameters:

r (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

Returns:

computed geometric factor

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.get_3d_param(param: str, pix_to_rsun: float, coefficients: ndarray, pixel_pos: list, sun_center_pos: list, z_pix: float) → float

Returns the electron density or temperature from a Metis map fitted with DDT. For the electron temperature calculation see utls.T_e_analytical.

Parameters:

  • param (str) – parameter to calculate, either Ne or Te

  • map_los (sunpy.map.Map) – map contaning the parameter and metadata in sunpy format

  • coefficients (np.ndarray) – [8, 360] array containing the fitted coefficients in the first axis.

  • pixel_pos (list) – [y, x] pixel coordinates at which to calculate the parameter.

  • sun_center_pos (list) – [y, x] pixel coordinates of the sun center. Provided by the user to avoid useless calculations.

  • x_los (float) – distance in pixels along the line of sight at which to calculate the parameter.

Returns:

electron density at set location

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.get_ne_map(filename)
doppler_dimming_lib.metis.get_pix_to_rsun(input_map: GenericMap) → float

Returns the platescale in pixel/Rsun

Parameters:

input_map (sunpy.map.Map) – map for which to calculate the ratio

Returns:

pixel/Rsun platescale

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.read_sav_model(filename: str) → list

Reads a sav idl file and returns the parameters list.

Parameters:

filename (str) – path to idl save file (.sav)

Returns:

list of 3d array containing the red parameters. In order: positions array, sun radial distances, sun latitude angle, densities, electron temperatures, proton temperatures, solar wind speed.

Return type:

list

doppler_dimming_lib.metis.resample_ddt_ne(new_dimension, ne_header, ne_data, ne_polar_data)

doppler_dimming_lib.parallelization.batch_2d_array(input_array, tot_nprocs)

doppler_dimming_lib.utils module

doppler_dimming_lib.utils.N_e_analytical(r: float) → float

Returns the electron density as a radial function from sun center.

Parameters:

r (float) – radial distance in R_sun

Returns:

electron density in cm-3

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.utils.T_e_analytical(rho: float, N_e_function=<function N_e_analytical>, **kwargs) → float

Returns an analytical electron temperature as a function of doppler_dimming_lim.utils.N_e_analytical from Lemaire and Stegen 2016

Parameters:

  • rho (float) – heliocentric distance in solar radii

  • N_e_function (callable) – density as a function of heliocentric distance (in sr). Use a polynomial to interpolate data if necessary

Returns:

electron temperature profile

Return type:

float

doppler_dimming_lib.utils.get_pix_to_rsun(input_map)
doppler_dimming_lib.utils.get_sun_center_from_map(map)

Returns the coordinates of the pixel containing the sun center. Use this instead of CRPIX!!!!

Parameters:

map (sunpy.map.Map) – map containing the image and reference frame

Returns:

coordinate (x, y)

Return type:

list

doppler_dimming_lib.utils.timeit(func)

Wrapper for timing functions’ execution times. Prints the difference between starting and ending time.

Parameters:

func (callable) – function of which to measure the execution time
